name: Triage inbound requests with Zendesk and Gmail with instant alerts

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09966-ticket`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Normalize Ticket Payload

### Normalize Ticket Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming ticket records to a stable schema

Next: Detect Sentiment

### Detect Sentiment

Scores the text's sentiment; ports fire positive / neutral / negative.

Ports: ①→ Escalate Negative — Gmail · ②→ Log Neutral Feedback, Capture Failure Context · ③→ Thank the Sender

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Google Gemini Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `models/gemini-2.5-flash`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Detect Sentiment** as `languageModel`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Escalate Negative — Gmail

gmail — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Open Zendesk Ticket · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Open Zendesk Ticket

zendesk — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Zendesk API token

### Log Neutral Feedback

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Thank the Sender

gmail — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Collect All Outcomes

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Guard Non-Empty subject

### Guard Non-Empty subject

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Gmail

### Escalate Failure to Gmail

gmail — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

# Triage inbound requests with Zendesk and Gmail with instant alerts

**Pattern:** Ticket Triage · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Triage inbound requests with Zendesk and Gmail with instant alerts. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual escalation is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Normalize Ticket Payload → Detect Sentiment → Escalate Negative — Gmail → Open Zendesk Ticket → Collect All Outcomes → Guard Non-Empty subject → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ticket triage without manual effort
- Anyone automating escalation in customer support
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google AI API key; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2; Zendesk API token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your escalation process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
